title: [phase:plan] Inspect the Codex task-completion backup path for performance and stuck-process risks. Focus on scan_codex_task_completions and record_explicit_completion behavior in /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py, plus any runtime scripts directly ca...
summary_text: Likely diagnosis (from code + runtime wiring): 1. `scan_codex_task_completions` has no wall-clock guard and can run arbitrarily long per invocation. It does a full session DB/filesystem walk, then performs dispatch/reconcile work in the same call, so one run can stay “busy” for a long time if external calls are slow or blocked. - scan definition: [task_backup.py]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:900 ) - dispatch/reconcile call inside scan: [task_backup.py]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:1512 ) / [task_backup.py]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:1710 ) 2. Each scanned completion event re-runs expensive helpers inside the loop: - `_codex_audit_snapshot` re-reads session JSONL for evidence. - `_recent_active_models` re-reads active-model log (`~/.codex/state/active-model-lanes.jsonl`) every event. That is potentially O(events × file-size) work and can create multi-minute runs before dispatch begins. - helper usage in scan: [task_backup.py]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:900 ) 3. `dispatch`/`reconcile` paths are network-bound and can be slow: each `_run_dispatch_command` has a 20s timeout, and scan/reconcile can run multiple commands (workflow/repo dispatch + contents/receipt checks) per record. With default batch 10, this alone can add substantial runtime per scan. - run command: [task_backup.py]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:1253 ) - defaults: [cli.py]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py:340 ) 4. Runtime scheduler is aggressive and can overlap/restart runs: launch agent defaults to `StartInterval=30` seconds and directly invokes the scan script every interval, while there is no dedicated scan lock skip/TTL. That can make one logical “scan” appear to persist/recur (or get terminated/restarted). - bootstrap interval: [bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/_worktrees/fugue-orchestrator-happy-kernel-landing-20260312/scripts/local/bootstrap-kernel-task-completion-backup-agent.sh:15 ) - scan runner path: [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/_worktrees/fugue-orchestrator-happy-kernel-landing-20260312/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:168 ) 5. `record_explicit_completion` is bounded by explicit sleeps totaling 26s for reconcile retries plus subprocess calls; less likely to explain 34m alone unless called repeatedly. It still shares the same dispatch/reconcile command path. - [task_backup.py]( /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py:981 ) What to confirm (fastest instrumentation): - Add per-phase timing in scan and reconcile/dispatch: - elapsed for: session walking, per-session audit, per-event `active-model` lookup, `_run_dispatch_command`, `_mark_*` updates, and total scan time. - Emit PID + start/end timestamps and “last SQL row”/“last file/offset” checkpoints in `journal_path` each run. - Add per-process heartbeat lock for scan to prevent overlap and skip when previous scan is still running. - Add `--max-scan-batch` / `--max-runtime-seconds` hard stops in scan CLI (or exit with clear “time slice exceeded” status). Minimal high-value fixes to prevent/mitigate 34-min stalls: - Introduce scan-level timeout/cap (e.g., 5–10 min runtime max and per-loop file/event caps). - Add in-script lock around scan path with stale-lock detection. - Add caching for per-session audit results within a single scan run. - Add DB/index checks and query plan review for pending/reconcile selectors (status/next-attempt lookups). - Consider increasing launcher interval or adding launchctl throttle + `KeepAlive` policy to avoid thrash if scan backs up. If you want, I can draft an exact patch plan (with specific code-level changes and log fields) for sc...
